title: Fix check-ai-pr to allow no-op archive restorations

sources.reason: The archive_evidence_changes function needs to exclude M-status files whose git blob is identical to HEAD (no-op restorations).
A new test must verify that no-op restorations (M with identical content) are allowed through.

intent.problem: When an archived Work Item file is accidentally modified in commit A, then restored to its original state in commit B, the CI check-ai-pr (base=A) sees the restoration commit as an M-status change to an archive file and fails with 'archive PR policy is append-only'. This is a false positive: the archive integrity is fully preserved (blob content unchanged from original). The current code has no way to distinguish a harmful tampering from a harmless no-op restoration.
constraints: The fix must not weaken the protection against actual archive tampering (content changes)
A genuine M with different content must still be rejected
The no-op detection must compare git blob hash of the file at base commit vs HEAD
The existing test test_pr_rejects_summary_only_tampering_even_when_new_work_item_claims_it must still pass
A new test must cover the no-op restoration case
intent.rationale: The append-only policy exists to protect archive evidence integrity, not to prevent restoring accidentally corrupted files to their correct state. A no-op restoration (M where blob at HEAD == blob at some earlier correct ancestor) should be treated as a pass-through since the net change to archive content is zero.

riskAssessment.reason: Targeted fix to a single check in ai_check_pr.py. The condition is conservative: only files where the git object hash is unchanged are excluded.

executionDecision.reason: Fix is well-scoped. Compare git blob at base commit vs HEAD for M-status archive files.

preReviewWarnings: Verify the no-op detection uses git object hashes, not file content comparison, to avoid subprocess overhead.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Single checkpoint sufficient for this focused fix.

acceptance: scripts/ai_check_pr.py: M-status archive files with identical blob to base commit are excluded from append-only violation
tests/test_pr_aggregate.py: new test verifies no-op restoration passes
guidelines: Use git cat-file or git show to compare blob hashes, not file content
Keep the change minimal and focused on the no-op detection only
Do not change the error message wording for genuine M violations

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: .ai/guards/coverage_policy.yaml requires adding a prAggregate association for scripts/ai_check_pr.py so the coverage guard recognizes tests/test_pr_aggregate.py as its test counterpart.

rollbackNote: Revert changes to scripts/ai_check_pr.py and tests/test_pr_aggregate.py.
